title: Controlled Playground Scenario
description: Single source of truth for one bounded, runnable docs playground. The same scenario file is consumed by the MDX playground component (interactive rendering) and by the Python validator (CI enforcement). A scenario replaces a static Python code block: it exposes a fixed code template plus an explicit, bounded control surface. Users may change only the declared controls within their declared limits. The two-layer boundary model is intentional: control min/max/values define the UI play range (soft boundary), while the SDK itself enforces correctness at runtime (hard boundary). The validator's job is to prove those two layers agree: in-range controls succeed, out-of-range controls raise the documented exception.

$schema.description: Optional path to this schema for editor validation.

id.description: Stable, unique scenario id. Referenced from MDX as <Playground scenario="..."> and used by the validator to report results.

title.description: Human-readable scenario title shown in the playground UI.

page.description: Docs page slug this scenario is embedded in, e.g. 'guides/quickstart'. Used to keep scenarios and pages traceable.

summary.description: One-line description of what this playground demonstrates.

runtime.description: Execution runtime. Only 'python' is supported today (browser-side via Pyodide in the UI, exec in the validator).

template.description: The fixed Python source. It is NOT user-editable. It executes with a pre-bound `params` dict whose keys are the control names; it must read every variable value from params[...] and must not contain hard-coded control values. This is the shared code that both the UI and the validator run.

locked.description: Human-readable list of the parts of the template users cannot change (imports, lifecycle order, call structure). Surfaced in the UI so limits are visible outside the code.

note.description: Reader-facing auxiliary description shown near the code, explaining in plain language what can be adjusted and what stays fixed.

explanations.description: Optional map of exception class name to a reader-facing explanation. When a run raises that exception, the playground shows the explanation beneath the error message, so a boundary outcome is self-explanatory and a dedicated failure section elsewhere becomes unnecessary.

controls.description: The bounded control surface. Each control maps a params key to a real SDK parameter or environment input, with explicit allowed values or range.

cases.description: The validated test matrix. Every scenario must declare at least one in-range success and one out-of-boundary failure.

valid.description: In-range control combinations that must execute successfully and match the expected output.

invalid.description: Out-of-boundary control combinations that must raise the documented exception.

name.description: Key used in params[...] inside the template and in every case's `controls` map.

type.description: Control kind. Determines which limit fields are required and how the UI renders it.

default.description: Default value used when the playground first renders. Must satisfy this control's own limits.

source.description: Where this control maps in the codebase, e.g. 'nano_llm.config.InferenceConfig.max_tokens' or the 'MOCK_RNGD_HARDWARE' env var. Keeps the control honest and traceable to real code.

description.description: Short explanation shown next to the control.

values.description: Allowed values. Required when type is 'enum'.

min.description: Inclusive lower bound. Required when type is 'int' or 'float'.

max.description: Inclusive upper bound. Required when type is 'int' or 'float'.

max_length.description: Maximum character length. Used when type is 'text'.

name.description: Short case id for validator reporting.

controls.description: Concrete in-range value for every control name.

name.description: Short case id for validator reporting.

controls.description: Concrete out-of-boundary value for at least one control.

status.description: 'enforced' = the SDK already raises this today. 'planned' = the boundary is designed but the SDK does not enforce it yet; the validator treats it as an expected-pending gap until SDK enforcement is added. This lets the contract land before the SDK changes.

stdout_contains.description: Every listed fragment must appear in captured stdout.

stdout_equals.description: Captured stdout must equal this exact string (after trailing-newline trim).

type.description: Expected exception class name, e.g. 'DeviceNotFoundError' or 'ValueError'.

message_contains.description: Substring that must appear in the exception message.
